rationale.R1.1: Strengthening the passage centrally involves sharpening the thesis, contribution claim, and framing of GoalAniso relative to the problem gap.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation requires checking whether the argument from anisotropic layer structure to DWR-based metric construction to practical benefit holds together.
rationale.R1.3: The performance claims and benchmark conclusions need to align with the reported experimental results to make the passage stronger.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes a strong first-algorithm claim while naming nearby prior work, so novelty positioning is central to the requested strengthening.
rationale.R1.6: The request naturally includes improving sentence-level force, clarity, concision, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad strengthening request must preserve uncertainty about unsupported or underspecified claims.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims such as first algorithm and practical option, so avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility is limited to projecting the delegation onto the responsibility space rather than executing revisions on the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Strengthening research prose must avoid creating misleading claims that could distort downstream scholarly interpretation.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to specific features of the provided delegation and artifact.
